verbatim_exchange.content: no in here in like I can**do** bold

Ah, got it! You're referring to using formatting like bold or italics within text here. In this interface, here’s what you can do:
•	Bold: **text** → text
•	Italic: *text* → text
•	Bold Italic: ***text*** → text
Unfortunately, cursive (like true script-style fonts) isn't supported directly in plain markdown formatting here. But if you're going for a cursive vibe or stylized text, I can help convert phrases into special Unicode cursive or script-style characters. For example:
•	Regular: Karen
•	𝓚𝓪𝓻𝓮𝓷 → using script font
•	𝒦𝒶𝓇ℯ𝓃 → a different variant
Would you like me to set up a converter or a reference sheet with different script styles for names or phrases?

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user inquired about specific text formatting capabilities within the current interface, including bold and cursive. The AI provided clear instructions for supported markdown formatting and offered an alternative Unicode-based solution for unsupported cursive styles, proactively offering further tools.
belief_implications: The user expects the AI to understand context-specific interface capabilities and limitations.
The user values clear, actionable instructions and creative alternatives when a direct request isn't feasible.

sensory_qualia.visual: Structured text, bullet points, examples of text transformations, distinct font styles for illustration.
sensory_qualia.haptics: Precise, deliberate typing, mental 'click' of understanding formatting rules.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: Attentive, focused on details, mentally navigating options.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: Steady, organized flow of information, clear presentation.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Informative, helpful, technically precise, structured learning environment.
